sections.paragraph:
fut. יַעֲנשׁ to fine, to impose a fine, to amerce. (Found besides only in the Rabbinic dialect. The primary idea appears to be that of imposing, laying upon; compare cogn. עָמַס, עָמַשׂ). Construed followed by לְ Pro 17:26 followed by two acc. to amerce any one in a sum of money, Deu 22:19 2Ch 36:3 (used of sums of money exacted in war); in wine, Amo 2:8. Impers. Pro 21:11, בַּעֲנָשׁ־לֵץ “when they (the judges) amerce the scoffer.”

Niphal, to be fined, Exo 21:22 gener. to be punished, Pro 22:3 27:12 .

[Hence the following words]-
